summary: Scorching Loser, soaring So Many Roads, deep Dark Star jam
blurb: The June 8 1992 show delivers a fiery Loser and an inspired So Many Roads. The second set builds with tight segues through China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider, Crazy Fingers → Corinna, and a powerful Dark Star. Overall the band is locked in, making it a standout performance of the 1992 tour.
review: Richfield Coliseum on June 8, 1992 captured one of the Dead’s high‑energy outings from the early ’90s. Jerry Garcia ripped through Loser with an intense solo and pushed his vocals forward on So Many Roads, while his guitar tone stayed bright throughout. Phil Lesh anchored the groove with moments of brilliance—though a few listeners noted a stumble during Stella Blue—and even took over lead vocals for So Many Roads, adding variety. Bob Weir’s rhythm work shone on the closing U.S. Blues, and with Brent absent, Vince Welnick supplied “smokin’” keyboards that textured the set without overwhelming it. The dual‑drum attack from Bill Kreutzmann (highlighted in the Drumz segment) and Mickey Hart was especially tight during the post‑Drums space, culminating in a soaring Stella Blue. Highlights include a seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition, a blistering Crazy Fingers → Corinna run, and an expansive Dark Star that many fans cite as the show’s centerpiece.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four of five recordings are marked positive with strong praise for Loser, So Many Roads, Dark Star and the second‑set flow; only one recording is mixed and none are negative, yielding a robust positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Clear soundboard source with balanced mix, solid guitar capture and minimal tape‑flip fades; it showcases the show’s highlights without major audio flaws.

key_highlights: Intense Loser solo and soaring So Many Roads performance
Seamless segues through China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider and Crazy Fingers → Corinna
Deep, exploratory Dark Star that stands out across both SBD and audience tapes
Strong second‑set momentum culminating in a powerful Stella Blue

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers an intense Loser solo, frenzied vocal work on So Many Roads and consistently active guitar; vocals occasionally sit too forward in the mix but overall tone is praised.
band_performance.Phil: Provides solid bass foundations with standout moments, steps up to sing So Many Roads, though a minority note a lapse (‘bombing’) during Stella Blue.
band_performance.Bob: Anchors the rhythm with a powerful U.S. Blues anthem and reliable guitar work throughout the set.
band_performance.Vince: Supplies ‘smokin’ keyboards that add rich texture without dominating, filling in for Brent’s absence.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums shine especially during the Stella Blue segment, delivering dynamic percussion alongside Bill.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann is highlighted in the Drumz portion and contributes tight, energetic drumming throughout the performance.
